reviews.review_description: I’ve played this game for about a week and a half…I’ve enjoyed it quite a bit! The visuals are great and there are SO MANY ways to tailor the game to your liking. I’d like to have more environments, but if not it’s fine because of the mixed reality mode. Also, I wish the robots wouldn’t get so close, but there’s probably a setting for that I’m overlooking. In any case, the game is structured in a way that makes you have to think, which is good for the mind and body. Will buy this game for a friend to enjoy to support the developer!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review.  If you send me a message on the meta horizon app to mus.magic, I'll be happy to share a free download code for your friend.  Regarding the environments, I had thought about that but it seemed it was just too dangerous to play without mixed reality mode (e.g. punching walls) so I decided there wasn't much value in it.  Please let me know if you feel strongly about it though and I'll see what I can do.  Regarding the robots getting to close, that was deliberate decision choice to force the player to move rather than fight stationary to help with the theme of making the game fitness focussed.  There is no option to change that at this time.  Please let me know if you think there should be and I'll work on it and thanks again for your feedback :-)

reviews.review_description: First thought it will be a game like thrill of the fight, but the movements of the opponent were totally real. It forces you to kove across the room. I am sweating just after one round. Kudos to the developers.

reviews.review_title: Great way to get you moving.
reviews.review_description:
This game is a great way to get your whole body moving. I liked how the faces of the robots gave feedback for punch strength.

Biggest positive: The way this game allows you to customize your experience puts every other fitness game to shame. You can tweak everything from your height to allowed movesets.  

Only real negative: The game requires a large clear space to really get the full experience with dodging and positioning against multiple opponents.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great feedback! I’m really glad the full-body movement, robot reactions, and customisation stood out. You’re right about the space requirement -it’s something I’m aware of, but it’s needed to play the game as intended and get the most out of the fitness experience. Thanks again for taking the time to leave a review, I really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: This is an outstanding project from an impressively small development team. The developer responds rapidly and individually to any feedback sent via email and it is so cool to see the game grow with everyone’s suggestions. The quality of the game is also impressively high. The dynamics definitely take some getting used to, but if you give yourself some time to learn how it works, this is a solid fitness game. It stands out from all other options especially due to its customizable features. Highly recommend!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the fantastic review! I’m glad you’ve enjoyed exploring all the customisation options - they’re designed to give everyone flexibility, and I’ve added tutorials and plan to make more videos and updates to help people get the most out of them. It really means a lot that you’ve taken the time to share your thoughts and recommend the game!

reviews.review_description: Great for staying active

reviews.review_title: Great game fun and great for exercise
reviews.review_description: I just started with this game, but I have to say not only is it fun to play is also a great workout game. This has practically replaced most of my exercise. The game play is very responsive. It runs very smooth and if I had to choose one game to be fun during myRoutines, then I highly recommend this game as with any workout game, you want to only do what you can handle I will say i've tried several games for exercise. And this one is not only good for exercise, it's also fun, check it out. I don't think you'll be disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your sharing your feedback - I’m so glad you’re enjoying it both as a game and a workout. That’s exactly what I was hoping for when I created Fight Fit VR

It’s great to hear it’s running smoothly for you as well. You’re absolutely right about pacing yourself and customising the game can really help get the most out of the workout.

reviews.review_description: Yeah did too much. It barely works now, cant land a punch, glitch heavy. Just feels messed up now

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for taking the time to leave a review. I’m really sorry to hear you’ve had a frustrating experience and don’t want anyone to feel that way about a game I have put so much care into. 

There haven’t been any major updates for several months, although that major update did change some gameplay mechanics, so it may feel different from before. The game has also been extensively playtested with other players, and I haven’t seen similar reports of punching or glitching issues. 

Because of all that, I suspect the problem is either specific to you in some way or some edge case I missed in the testing. 

I'd love to work with you directly to get to the bottom of it. If you're open to it, please reach out to me at fightfitvr@gmail.com and I'll investigate with you. In the meantime, I'm also able to roll you back to an earlier version of the game if that would help.

Look forward to hearing from you

reviews.review_title: 🥊Intense Arcade Boxing for a good sweat
reviews.review_description:
Fight Fit VR is a fun and energetic arcade fighting game where you face waves of robot opponents and often find yourself outnumbered. Precision and power really matter here—the game clearly distinguishes between light and heavy punches, which makes every strike feel meaningful.

Cardio and energy management are just as important as strength. Each round lasts a full 3 minutes, just like a real boxing match, so you need to pace yourself if you want to survive to the end. It’s a great workout disguised as a game, pushing both your reflexes and endurance. The enemies also are getting stronger and stronger

Packed with action and non-stop punching, Fight Fit VR delivers plenty of fun while getting your heart rate up. A very enjoyable experience for anyone looking for an active and satisfying VR + MR boxing game.
I really liked the boxer models and how they shadow the punch before they throw it! Hell of a workout!
I'm really enjoying the gameplay with 2 enemies. The importance on movement and positioning involved is not something I've seen in other similar games i've played.

Hey dev!  Wanted to give you a decent review and some feedback as you have some promise here I think.

Your game is not bad, but needs some polish.

Here are a few things I remember thinking while playing it.

1. The talking when you hit someone is a bit excessive.  

2.  Unless you have a wide area having people drop 180 degrees from the other thing you are is frustrating.  

And most people don’t have huge rooms to play in. If you really want it this way, I think you need to give some better cues kind of like how they do it in the Quest Batman game.  

3.  I feel like hamstringing the main character by making blocking something they have to turn on is frustrating.  In real life you block or at least put up your guard.  It’s pretty crucial.  Dodging is only part of the equation. 

I feel like you really need blocking to be a primary point of the game especially when some opponents kick which gives them an advantage.  Plus blocking is fun.  Gameplay is fun when you have options.  

When playing Thrill of the Fight, I kept playing because I kept intuitively learning new ways to win.  

At first it was moving around, then slipping, then blocking and slipping, then ducking.  That’s part of the game longevity.  

Maybe your character learns to block or something?  If you made it feel like these new “skills” will be learned, I could understand that, but the game isn’t easy, so give your players the ability to succeed.

4.  The blood splatter is a bit excessive.  Removes the immersion.  Maybe just a sound or make things look foggy with haptics for a second.  

Ok hope this helps.  Good job!  Keep going.

I just posted this then saw your latest update notes.  Great to hear you’re listening to your players. 👍

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to share such a detailed review—really appreciate the feedback and your encouragement!

You're absolutely right about the announce chat during hits. I had a hunch it might get annoying fast 😂 so I added an option in the settings to tone it down.

Great point about enemy spawn angles too. I did implement audio cues, but based on your feedback I’ll have enemies spawn closer to where the player is actually looking in the next update.

On blocking: My original idea was to design a fitness game through fighting game mechanics so I wanted to favour movement and dodging over stationary blocking.  Please note the setting to turn on blocking is auto-saved unless the difficulty gets changed through the buttons (which load predefined presets). Also, whilst blocking is an option, parrying is always available regardless of settings, and personally, I find it more fun than blocking.

And that feedback on the blood effect is spot-on. I looked into screen shake but that tends to make people queasy in VR, so I’ll be swapping the blood splatter out for a fog effect with haptics in the next update. 

Best regards

mus.magic

edit - Just to let you know the latest update (and hopefully last major update) addresses point 2 and 4.  For point 2, there is now audio cues for where enemies land and for when an attack is coming but it not within the player's field of view.  They also should always respawn within the player's field of view.  For point 4, i have replaced the blood spatter effect with a light might and if the enemy attack is strong enough, a bit of screen shake.  Many thanks for highlighting these points and would be great to get your thoughts if you are still finding problems.  if you do, i am available at fightfitvr@gmail.com .

reviews.review_title: Why change it to require internet connec
reviews.review_description: This game used to be a must have for me. I give it to friends that come around but since it requires internet access, we can no longer enjoy it during hangouts.

developer_response.body: So sorry for the delay.  took much longer than expected to get the last update out.  It should now work without an internet connection.  Can you please give it a go when you have time and let me know if there are any problems.  many thanks

reviews.review_description:
I never used￼ it pretty boring waste of my money I already know how to fight things just a waste of my money so I want a refund. It’s so boring. It has nothing to do it. All you do is try to fight. It’s very boring and it wastes a lot of my storage.
￼￼￼￼

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback - though it sounds like you may not have actually played the game based on your comment “I never used it”.  Can you please clarify.
  
If you have played it, it would be very helpful please to get your thoughts on what you were expecting of a $7 fitness game built on fighting game mechanics.  As a solo dev that has sunk so much of my time into it, it is very important to me to ensure it meets reasonable expectations when it comes out of early access.  

Whilst we are all entitled to our opinions, I think your comment on file size is unfair.  The average on the Meta Quest is 2-3GB.  Fight Fit VR is 250MB. 

Btw – in case you were not aware, Meta’s refund policy allows returns within 14 days and under 2 hours of playtime so if you haven’t played it yet, you should be able to get your money back.

reviews.review_description:
Reason for 4 star is sometimes the hits don't register. Secondly, the counter punch mission is extremely annoying, I counter but it doesn't register. It needs to be removed. And only because of the counter punch mode I lose about 75% health and it just destroys the experience. I don't want to start all over again for a function in your game which doesn't work properly.

Edit - thanks for the lengthy reply. I appreciate you trying to do your best for the community and putting in a lot of effort. 

A visual cue would be a really good addition. Make it an option that people can turn off if they are comfortable with the counter attacking. I don't think making the counter window would be a good idea as the attack speed of the AI can be changed. Or remove the counter punching mechanic altogether. 

Another thing that would make the experience better is if im in a defensive stance and i know a punch is coming, I am already blocking it before it lands. The game only allows blocks if I actually move my hand towards the attacking punch in order to block it. If I have my guard up before the punch lands, it should be classed as a block

It is a fantastic game and I get a really good workout which is the aim for me.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to leave a detailed review — I really appreciate it, and I’m sorry to hear that the counter punch mode caused frustration.

Just to give a bit of context: originally, the counter window started at the very beginning of the enemy's attack animation, but that ended up making countering far too easy. Based on feedback, I shifted the timing so that a counter now only registers during the moment the enemy can actually deal damage - for example, if the enemy steps forward and then punches, your counter needs to land during the punching part, not during the step. That said, I completely understand that it’s still causing some difficulty.

Would a visual cue - like the enemy flashing briefly when they're vulnerable to a counter - help make it feel more intuitive? Or would you prefer if the counter window itself were simply made longer? I’d love to hear your thoughts, and I’ll work your feedback into the next update (which is due out in the next month or two).

On punch detection: you’re right, it’s one of the trickiest technical challenges in VR. Right now, my approach is to measure controller velocity to determine whether something is a punch, and each fist has a brief cooldown (about 0.25s) after registering a hit, to avoid unintentional multi-hits. I’ve tested a range of methods, but this approach has performed best so far. That said, it’s not perfect - especially in cases where your punch might graze an enemy’s arms first, as that can prevent the hit from reaching the intended target zone.

To improve this further, I’ve started work on a machine learning model that will help classify punch types, strength, and intent with more nuance - though I need around 30,000 real samples to train it properly. If you’re open to contributing to that dataset, I’d be happy to pay for your time. You can reach me at fightfitvr@gmail.com if you're interested.

Last thing, to improve the game, beyond the counter punch and punch detection mechanics, what else do you feel would bring Fight Fit VR up to a 5-star experience in your eyes? I want to make this the best it can be and would love your insight.
Thanks again for your honesty - it genuinely helps shape where the game goes next.

Edit - apologies for the delayed response, i didn't see your edit for my response. Many thanks for the suggestions, i'll work it into the next update.  btw - for the blocking, i wanted to keep the focus of the game on fitness and hence the default setting is no blocking but instead the player can parry by punching away the enemy attack.  However, adding player blocking was the number one requested feature so I added it in the last update.  you can find it in the options.  Let me know if you can't find it and i'll put up a video on youtube to demonstrate for you.

Edit 2 - So sorry it took a while but I finally got the update out which amongst a whole bunch of other stuff, has made counter punch mode easier.  I didn't go with the visual cue though, let me know if you feel it needs it and i'll add it.  Note there is now a poise system which means some enemy attacks will continue despite being hit by the player which may give the impression of hit detection being off.  This behaviour can be dialled up or down in the options.  Please let me know if there are still hit detection problems though.  I'm at fightfitvr@gmail.com if you want to discuss anything else you see further.  I would love to hear your thoughts :-)

reviews.review_description: Simply brilliant. Awesome graphics. I’ve studied martial arts for years with quite a few styles, with the blocking mode on this is an awesome way to practice block/attack combos for Wing Tsun. Great to be in front of some legendary looking opponents. Amazing workout too!!

It is very good.  I am a former TKD instructor now disabled and use the app to keep going.  I have trouble with "head and body only" because it doesn't recognize my targets, which are head and body only.  otherwise, very challenging.  Works very well sitting or in a wheelchair.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review and feedback! I'm very happy to hear that Fight Fit VR is helping you stay active.

Regarding the "head and body only" punch mode, at this time punches need to land cleanly on the head or body to register. If there's any contact with the enemy’s arms or legs beforehand, the hit will not be recognized. I’m working on improving this so that partial contact—such as brushing past the enemy’s arms—doesn't interfere with recognizing a hit to the intended target.  I think this maybe a month or two away though.

If you have any other thoughts on how to improve the game, please email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com

reviews.review_description:
The game is on the way to become greater than TotF, which is good but is not a very high bar.

Here enemies actually move like martial artists, there is a working notion of dodging and counterpunching. Sometimes you can read opponent’s body language (which TotF has no notion at all). 

There is a long way to go, though, to become a really great fighting sim.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your feedback, I really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts and I'm glad to hear there are aspects of the game that stood out to you.
While the game does lean into fighting mechanics, my main goal from the beginning has been to create a fitness game rather than a fighting simulator. The combat is there to keep players moving, challenged, and immersed. That said, due to player requests, I’ve been building on the fighting mechanics but still have some way to go. I think it's important to listen to the Fight Fit VR player community to make changes that cater to both types of player. The customisation options will hopefully allow for people to enjoy a great workout and get the best out of the game. For example, I recently added blocking even though it goes against the fitness principles. 
To this end, when you have time, it would be fantastic if you could email please at fightfitvr@gmail.com to get your thoughts on where the fighting mechanics can be improved.

reviews.review_title: I want to like this game…
reviews.review_description: I love the concept of this game and the mix VR, but my progress is not registering. I have completed two consecutive rounds of 3 mins with a score of over 2,000 for each round (several times) and the game still will not unlock features for me even tho I met the requirements. Help !

developer_response.body:
I am so sorry you the unlocks are not working for you.  Another player contacted me a few weeks ago with the same problem.  I tried to replicate their problem but couldn't and also from the developer dashboard, I am seeing new players unlock the other game modes so asked them some questions to help me debug it but never heard back from them so assumed it was ok (and breathed a sigh of relief!).  Are you playing with an internet connection as if not, the unlocks system does not work.  Also, can you check what achievements you have (the menu on the right has an achievements button), do you see the 2 rounds and 2k with a tick next to them?  Can you please let me know on fightfitvr@gmail.com.  Also, if you are able, can you please send me a video of you doing two rounds and getting over 2k score together with what options you have selected.  Same for anyone else having the same problem please to help me debug it.  Btw - I really appreciate you bringing this to my attention, if you see dlc in the future (i have a couple of things planned), please get in touch and i'll send you a free download code.  

Best regards.  

Mus.magic

edit - with the latest update, I changed some of the unlock rules including that no internet connection is required.  many thanks for flagging the problem.  When you get time, can you please try it out and let me know if you are still having problems.   many thanks

reviews.review_title: Great game - well worth the price!
reviews.review_description:
I've been searching for a game to scratch that ToTF (Thrill of the Fight) itch. Something that can keep me going and give me a good HIIT or cardio workout. I've tried other boxing games - Mutant League Boxing, Creed, etc.. and most of them are good! but they are way too "arcade" focused. Fight Fit VR however, is more fitness focused.

What does that mean for you? If you're looking for a real fitness workout, something that will get the heart racing and you moving, this is it. You feel like you're being attacked by multiple robot assailants and they feel "alive".  
The *only* caveat I will say (and I'll echo another reviewer) is the note that unless you're in augmented reality mode, it's very easy to hit a wall, even if you have the recommended play space. I do hope that there is (or will be) a way to customize the area and fight towards the center ala ToTF where the arena can be shrunk down and the opponents somehow come from inside the center rather than outside the play area (because my first reaction is to "reach out and box them". But the Augmented Reality mode works perfectly, so no real complaints.

The early access price is a steal, and if you're looking for something that will get your heart rate up like ToTF does - this is for you.

This is a great game, and I only hope the dev is successful so they can add more content and such!!

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks so much for your review! I’m really glad you’re enjoying the game and it’s giving you the workout you were looking for, this is exactly what I wanted the game to do! It means a lot to hear that it is standing out and gives you that real fitness feel.
I decided to keep the play space the same so that scoring stays fair for everyone. Also, in smaller spaces, things got way too hectic when fighting two enemies- it just didn’t feel right. That’s why it’s set up the way it is.

To help with risks of hitting the wall, I’ve updated the instructions to include how to set up your guardian boundary properly. It’s not a perfect solution, but I’m really hoping most players enjoy and stick with the mixed reality mode!

I’m still actively working on the game, and there’s lots more to come - like player avatars, new game modes, updated enemy graphics, better AI, and more customisation options. Plus, I’ve got DLC on the way.
Let me know once you see any DLC, I’d be happy to send you a free download code for it.

reviews.review_description:
1. Go to my driveway  
2. Fire up this game  
3. Fight off waves of enemies until I'm drenched  
4. Shower  

Thanks, developer.  Fantastic game.  And you can probably remove the "blood" ESRB designation, since I didn't see any.

reviews.review_title: Tried them all,this is the best !!
reviews.review_description:
Tried so many boxing apps but as an older gamer/vr this is for me.Sick of enemies spamming your face?Fight Fit Vr has so many tunable options i can tailor the enemies just how i like speed intensity ect.Realise some may prefer more challange but please keep it as an option as this is the biggest draw for me in this game.Mixed reality works great(indeed is almost essential)
thanks!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your review. As an older gamer myself, I’m really happy you get what I was trying to do with the game :-) there is still more to come with the game, including better enemy ai so I hope to be able to incorporate more of a challenge for those that want that too. 

Best regards

Mus.magic

reviews.review_description:
WARNING! ⚠️⚠️⚠️ only play this game in augmented reality mode!!!

Unlike other fight games, the opponents come from outside the play area. And there isn't a strong warning if you start punching outside the boundary. So, on my first game, I broke my finger punching a wall!

That said, with AR mode on, it is a great game and one of my favourites since Thrill of the Fight 1. I like the fact that it is more duck and weave with high intensity bursts than standard boxing. So it is a great complement to Thrill of the Fight.

I would have given 5 stars, but my finger is still really sore a few weeks later! 

To the developers: Can you please default to AR mode for safety or provide a warning? I'm not being soft or woke, I'm just keen for people to have fun, get fit, while mot hurting themselves. Ps I have a quest 2!

developer_response.body:
I'm so sorry to hear about your finger, and I really appreciate you taking the time to share your experience and recommendations. Player safety is incredibly important to me, which is why the game has a third of the main UI devoted to safety recommendations on start up. Based on your feedback, I'll work on providing additional guidance in those safety recommendations to clarify players should set their boundary in a way that takes into account their arm length to improve visibility of the guardian warnings before the player punch rather than after they have punched. I'll also explore ways to encourage players to opt for AR mode from the start.

Thank you so much for your ideas, and it makes me truly happy to hear you're enjoying the game! If you have any additional thoughts or suggestions, please do reach out at fightfitvr@gmail.com

reviews.review_description:
This is a pretty good exercise game, but as a combat game it lacks physics, e.g., your hands often pass through your enemies. 
Question to developers: Is there a way to block attacks? If not I hope you could really work on that...

UPDATE:
Hey thank you so much for replying!! Yeah let me clarify what I meant by "physics". I can literally walk through enemies as if they were a ghost, which makes a pretty realistic game all of a sudden fell fake. I'm more of a grappler than a striker in real life so I'm used to get up close. Would it be possible for my enemies to either step back/sideway, get pushed back, or clench if I get up close? I don't know how hard it is to program this.

But my biggest wish is to be able to block attacks, which I'm glad to hear that you're working on! I'm thinking something like all enemy attacks would be nullified if I block them with my hands or forearms. Really looking forward to the next update.

Oh and one more suggestion, if I may... Would it be possible to limit the enemies to move only within the play area that I drew? I spend quite a bit of time just waiting for them to move into the playable area.

I really like the fluid motions, and this app is definitely a keep.

developer_response.body:
Many thanks for the feedback.  To help me improve the flaws before coming out of early access, can I kindly ask if you can email me them at fightfitvr@gmail.com.  

Regarding physics, I have spent a lot of time trying to implement physics based reactions but the end result always looked inferior to animated reactions.  That said I do plan on trying again in the near future.  Can I ask please what physics based reactions you were expecting besides hands not going through enemies?

On the topic of hands passing through enemies, that should not happen, are you able to send me a video of where you seeing this please?  I coded it so that your virtual hands will not go through the enemy but as this can cause your real and virtual hands to go out of sync, if they go too far apart, your virtual hand will snap back to where your real hand is.  As such, your comment indicates there may be a bug I haven't seen before and need to address.

Regarding blocking, I did not include this as I wanted the game to be about fitness through fighting game mechanics rather than a fighting game with a fitness side effect.  As such, I though not having blocking would encourage more movement and so more fitness.

However, it is the number one requested feature so I am working on it at the moment and it should be out for the next update which is due in two or weeks.  I'm working on a parry system as the default as a compromise but with optionality for normal blocking also there although the latter incurs a scoring penalty.  Would be great to get your thoughts on it when it is ready.

many thanks

mus.magic

Update
apologies for the late reply, only just saw your update and clarifying the physics point.  I'll get working on this soon but I suspect it will be a while before I can have it ready unfortunately due to how hard I've found it to make the reactions looks realistic.  At the moment, if it helps, the enemies do step back when you get too close but these is when they are in an idle state only.  

In regards to choosing the play area that you draw, I've thought about this and was thinking instead to just make the arena smaller as a way of ensuring everyone plays the same game as far as possible for leaderboard purposes.  e.g. if someone has a smaller arena, the game is harder.    what do you think?

edit - in the latest update, there is now an option for player blocking.  please note it is turned off by default.  also there is a player avatar and more work done into preventing the player or the enemy going through each other but it is not completely eliminted though.  Would be great to get your thoughts on the update when you have time.  many thanks

reviews.review_title: A fun little app.  Great Cardio
reviews.review_description:
For its price, it's great.  I tried the demo first and was hooked.
I generally use the fight mode as part of my exercise routine, so my review is based solely on my experience in fight mode.
There's a variety of different options to suit beginners, all the up to seasoned pros, and sadistic little freaks like me who play 9 minutes per round.
It's best played in MR, I dread to think what my hands would look like in VR mode😅
The dev is willing to listen to people and make suitable changes.
I recommend to anyone looking for a challenging yet fun way to exercise with the Quest.

developer_response.body: I’m so happy to hear you are enjoying the game and it’s become part of your workout routine! I’ve added lots of new options to make the game even more customisable so hope you have found them helpful! I’m always looking of ways to improve the game and feedback from gamers like yourself is invaluable! I plan to release DLC in the future - if you see it, please email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com and I’ll to send you a free download code.

reviews.review_description: This game gets my heart rate up and moves my body! The voice in the latest update is much easier to understand! I still don't understand all of the terms and I can't figure out how to dodge most of the attacks, but there are lots of different options and settings to help adjust it to my liking. The developer seem open to suggestions and comments. I'm confident this game will continue to improve!

developer_response.body:
Many thanks for your updated feedback and really happy the new announcer is clearer :-)  In regards to the terms I think you are referring to the play modes.  I'll look into improving the UI and tutorials to make it clearer.  In summary, it is was designed to add variety to the game by modifying what player punches are registered.  For example, punches to the head only, strong punches only, etc. However, based on your feedback and that from others, I think it may be best to remove it from being applied as default and instead have it as an option.  What do you think?

In regards to dodging, it can be difficult. In the options menu to the left of the main menu, you can enable the foresight option and reduce enemy speed, this should make it easier for you to evade enemy attacks.  I'm also thinking of adjusting the contact logic to ignore or reduce the impact of partial enemy attack contact (e.g. to differentiate between a solid contact and an enemy attack that just brushed the player).  I'm hoping this too will make dodging easier. 

In regards to the rest time between rounds I added this based on player feedback to provide a recovery period to mimick real life exercises / boxing rounds.  However, if it is not for you, in the above mentioned options you can increase round time and reduce rest time between rounds.    

If you have any other feedback, please do let me know either here or at fightfitvr@gmail.com and thanks again for taking the time to help me improve the game :-)

edit - I just released a big update.  Would be great to get your thoughts on it when you have time.  many thanks

reviews.review_description: I love this game. Perfect for building up your CV fitness as well as toning. Recommend mixed reality for moving and throwing punches.

reviews.review_title: Basic and early, but does what it says.
reviews.review_description:
It's basic and because it's not like other boxing games the UI felt overwhelming until i searched up the youtube tutorial,but the core being built from is solid and worth spending time in already. 

2 enemies at the start of every go and no blocking is a pretty intimidating opening experience, but once you dial in the difficulty it feels great to no nonsense get into a session. Highly recommended and i look forward to seeing early access progress.

Edited above to be clearer for the dev's benefit and also: nothing to be sorry for! I'm no ui expert but i hope that was more constructive than the original wording.

developer_response.body:
Many thanks for your feedback.  In regards to the game play, I was going for a fightness game through fighting game mechanics as opposed to the other way around so it is not a deep fighting simulator by design and geared towards making the player move but in a fun way.  That said, I would love to hear your thoughts on how it can beyond basic whilst sticking to the theme and if I can, i'll put it in the game.  

Regarding the UI, I apologise for this, I'm not artistically gifted so have this on my to do list to spruce up before coming out of early access.  I had thought it was at least functional for now but if you have any ideas on what it should look like and how it should operate, would you be interested in working together to redesign it?  We can come to some arrangement to compensate you for your time.

reviews.review_description: It’s awesome. As a person who is in martial arts i love it. I’d just suggest being able to chose if the enemy uses both arms and legs also the player not only dodging but actually being able to block the punches. Nevertheless it’s a very good exercise 👊🏼!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the feedback and review! No problem, I’ll add the option to choose between punches or kicks to the to do list (probably about a month or two). 
Regarding blocking, the game is designed to be a fitness game through fighting game mechanics as opposed to the other way around and so my intention was to force the player to move as much as possible and removing blocking seemed like a good way to do that. However, this has come up so much that I’ve decided to add it.  It’s a big change to how I designed the game though so it is unlikely I can add the option any time soon unfortunately so may have to wait till the sequel/dlc.  
If you have any other feedback please do let me know at fightfitvr@gmail.com.

reviews.review_description: Once you understand that you need to use the room space and your feet the game works great. Like in real boxing you need to step side to side, forward and back to dodge the opponents. I love how you actually fight 2 enemies as the same time, literally keeps you on your feet. You do work a sweat so if you want to play a game that's going to burn some calories give it a go.
Amazing game

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the feedback, it really makes me happy to hear you are enjoying the game. It’s still in early access, with lots of new features being added as I continue to develop the game to hopefully make it even better! I plan to release DLC in the future- when its out, please email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com and I will happily send you a free download code.

reviews.review_description:
This game is superb. You did great work. I know thats only early Access, but I see potential.
 I popose add option with parry punches, example switch in menu.
Will be more enemies and hits to dodge ?

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review! It truly makes my day when a player not only recognizes the effort, but also shares the same vision for the experience I’m trying to create. 

Be sure to keep an eye out for updates - I’m hoping to release one later today with a few small improvements to the AI and the introduction of an exercise mode, along with some other enhancements.

Regarding the extra attacks and enemies, I'm planning to add back improved kung fu and karate as well as more attacks for advanced players for the existing fight styles.  However, please note Karate and Kung Fu will likely be DLC, I'll PM you the free download code when it is released. Also, could you clarify what you mean by parry punches? I want to make sure I understand so I can address it properly.

If you’re referring to enemies parrying the player’s punches, I did experiment with this feature, but my initial attempts to sync the enemy’s parry with the player’s punch didn’t work as expected. However, I can certainly try again.

If you’re talking about the player parrying enemy punches, that’s actually possible - it happens when the player’s punch connects with the enemy’s punch.

If you come across any bugs or have suggestions for features you’d like to see before coming out of early access (about a month or two), please reach out to me at fightfitvr@gmail.com. I would love to hear your thoughts.

Thanks again :-)

reviews.review_description: I was not sure if I will like the game when I purchased it.  It turned out to be very fun.  I would like to see a mode where an ai opponent performs one type of attack or combinations continually.  In that way, one can practice defensive movements and counter attacks.  Another suggestion is to add more variety of kicks like front kick, side kick, back kick, heel kicks in karate mode to ai attack options.  It would be cool to add ai weapon attacks, too.  Grappling and throws will go against the purpose of the game though, perhaps another game.

developer_response.body:
Hiya, thanks so much for the review and the suggestions, i'll add the which attacks option in training mode to the list of updates i'm working on.  

For the other idea for more kick variety for the karate guy, unfortunately I will be swapping karate and kung fu for power puncher and MMA very soon.  I had the same concerns about the number of attacks they had plus the attacks they were doing were quite hard to dodge and counter.  If i can get my hands on more decent kung fu and karate attack animations, i'll add them back in the future. 

Re weapons, I had tried it where the enemies have weapons but the player doesn't.  didn't work out unfortunately as it was very hard to land attacks on the enemy due to the extra range they have from their weapon and the constraints on play space most people have.  That said, I am working on a knife guy as a round ten boss who has a couple of one hit kills.  I'm hoping with a short range weapon like a knife, the range/play space won't be an issue.

For grappling and throws, spot on re it being against the purpose of the game as it would slow down the tempo and hence cardio benefit.  I will try adding a push ability though where the player can push the enemy back although I have concerns that too would slow down the tempo.

Thanks again for your ideas.  If you have any others, please email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com

ps. i'll send out a notification soon about the update as there is also enemy ai blocking in there too plus a bunch of fixes and small changes.  would be great to get your thoughts on it.

Best regards

Mus.Magic

reviews.review_description:
I've been playing Fight Fit VR for a few days now and it's become a staple in my fitness routine. The absence of blocking really forces you to move and facing two enemies at once are great design choices as it makes it intense and a great cardio workout. 

Tracking my progress through how many rounds can i last and my leaderboard rank keeps me coming back and the accessibility options and customization settings is a nice touch

For a first solo-developed indie game, it's pretty impressive. The graphics might not be top-notch, but the design choices make up for it.  Don't think I've come across another quest game that hits the sweet spot of fun fighting and cardio quite like this does. Definitely recommend it.

developer_response.body: really happy you like my game and get my vision for how the game is meant to be played and enjoyed.  The game is still in early access so if you have any thoughts on how to improve the game, found any bugs, etc, it would be greatly appreciated if you can email me on fightfitvr@gmail.com or fill out the feedback form here https://www.fightfitvr.com/feedback

reviews.review_description: Although I am sweating like crazy I am having the time of my life

developer_response.body: That’s great to hear and exactly what I aimed for with this game - a great workout that’s lots of fun! Keep up the good work!

reviews.review_description:
Very good and intensive game.
In addition there is some block features needed, only dodge and punch is little simple.
Andanced option of traning is good, some kind of MMA style is missing, boxing, kickboxing, karate and Kung-fu.

developer_response.body:
Hi Pavel200sx63, happy holidays and best wishes to you and your family :-) 

Thanks so much for your review and feedback.  It's invaluable to solo devs such as myself in helping improve the game before coming out of early access. Great idea regarding the MMA style, i'm thinking something like the boxer with kick attacks as well.   What do you think?

Regarding simplicity, I was concerned about that so I added the playmodes that you can choose to add to the first half of every round as well as the  automatically applied playmodes that occur in the last half of every round.  For example, first round boss dismisses punches to the enemy's guard, you can only punch the head and body. I was hoping this would give the game the variety and depth it needs but will continue thinking of more ways to mix it up.  If you have any ideas, please do email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com.

Regarding blocking, I made a decision early on that taking out blocking would force the player to move more which increases the cardio benefit.  I just created a poll to track feedback on this point -> http://youtube.com/post/UgkxTAZFM_fCyL-frLn9C-iU51oPpJaaJmN8?si=Ve7mydmMV1DckUx3.  It would be greatly if you could please vote when you have a  moment.

Thanks again for your feedback buddy

mus.magic

reviews.review_title: Can’t get enough of this  game
reviews.review_description: My family can’t get enough of this game. This is game is a lot of fun and a great work out for the entire at the same time. My kids are very energetic and it’s a great way for them to channel their energies. My wife and I  love playing the game as well, and get a fantastic work out after a few rounds of playing this game

I've  really loved playing this game- it's really great for people of many different abilities when it comes to fighting. It's perfect for exercising and really gets you sweating. It's become something I look forwards to and never get bored of. I definitely recommend and don't regret getting at all.

This is the closest game to a real fight in VR. Just 7 minutes on the medium difficulty made me sweat as if I had run several km. It’s great entertainment and an excellent workout. Game is a perfect example of how VR can be used for training.

reviews.review_title: Perfect for Boxing Training and Fitness!
reviews.review_description:
Great game! I stumbled across it while searching for ways to improve my boxing, and it’s exactly what I needed. As someone who trains in boxing, I really appreciate the options to focus on specific skill sets like dodging, counterpunching, and targeting the body or head. It’s a lot of fun, simple to use, and gets the job done. Even if you don’t train boxing, this game is a great way to burn calories, and you can adjust the intensity to suit your level. Highly recommend!

I’d like to highlight that it also features mixed reality, which is a huge plus it makes the game more immersive and much safer, as it ensures you won’t accidentally hit the wall.

developer_response.body: Many thanks for the review and really happy you like the game.  You are the first person to point out the play modes which I felt was needed to give the game more variety and depth.  If you have any other ideas on how to improve the game, please email me at fightfitvr@gmail.com

reviews.review_description:
Just bought this and like it so far!

I would love an endless option without timed rounds to increase cardio, a God mode where you can fight continually without sustaining major damage, and ability to add numerous opponents simultaneously in both training and rounds.

Great job!

developer_response.body: Many thanks for your review and suggestions.  If you have any more, please don't hesistate to reach out at fightfitvr@gmail.com.  Can i check please whether you have tried adjusting the settings to the left of the main menu.  For example, there are options there to increase player health, reduce enemy attack damage, increase round length, reduce rest length, etc.  In combination, this should give you the god effect.  Re training having more than one enemy, that is definitely doable and will put it on the to do list before coming out of early access.

reviews.review_description: I don't think I've had as much fun playing any game, than this Fight Fit VR! Its exhilarating and keeps you in your toes boxing baddies. Really cool punch tricks and blocking, and undercuts does the job. Such a great workout too. I'm literally sweating after every game. I've tried this with both quest2 and quest3, see through with Q3 is great. Overall I give this game 5stars. Definitely on of the best game.

developer_response.body: Many thanks for your review and really appreciate the five stars.  However, just wanted to clarify for anyone else reading this review, there is no blocking in this game and this is a deliberate design choice to force the player to physically move to evade attacks.

reviews.review_description: I've been really enjoying Fight Fit VR. It's unique and different from other VR games I've played. The barriers to entry are low, making it accessible to people that aren't boxing masters like myself. The leaderboard feature is great and has motivated me to keep improving. I also love the Mixed Reality mode—it's really awesome. The no-blocking mechanic is a fantastic touch, as it forces you to move around and get a good workout.
it gets so much more fun as you turn up the difficulty! cant wait to see what else is added to the game before general release
Really challenging game, makes me work up a sweat! The kids love it too
really makes you sweat in a fun and addictive way, and for a fiver, it's an absolute bargain
its so fun knocking down bad guys!

reviews.review_title: Proper fun and a great workout
reviews.review_description: Proper fun and a great way to stay active! You’re in a fight arena punching bad guys as they spawn, and it gets harder longer you ￼lost. It’s really fun, but you’ll build up a proper sweat without realising - my heart’s really pumping towards the end! Try to beat your own high score or get on the leaderboard, if you want to find a way to work out without hitting the gym, this is spot on! ￼
